reviews.review_msg: very knowledgeable, prepare to have your perspective changed. If you are passionate about studying Feminism, Racism, Colonialism ect. this is the class for you. WITH THAT SAID this is by no means an easy course. Attendance is taken, readings are mandatory, & assignments are written in class to avoid AI. Can be a tough grader, but she is wonderful.

She is such an intelligent individual and I could listen to her lectures all day. Definitely a difficult marker though with some murky grading where I wasn't sure what she was looking for - overall, super interesting prof and clearly loves her job + teaching!

The grading criteria for this class was all over the place. She is a very tough grader and will give similar grades to very different levels of work. She does want everyone to understand the material and gives an engaging lecture, but I just was not a fan of this class and the way it was graded.

She's a great lecturer and clearly very passionate - but do not take her class if you're looking for an easy A, she's a difficult marker and expects nuance and great understanding in your writing. All assignments are in-class for the most part, as she is very anti-AI (thank god)

She is an incredibly intelligent woman who is very passionate about what she's teaching - very magnetic to listen to. She's a hard marker but she's incredibly inspirational, cuts to the chase, and has very engaging lectures that will never put you to sleep.

Mariam is an amazing prof, she cares about her students and their learning. The assignments and in class essays weren't hard but you do need to do the readings or you will fall behind in discussions. Overall great course, and the lectures are very informative and engaging. the only down side is that its a 3 hour lecture so not everyone likes that

In person, no technology, hand written papers were difficult, but reasonable. Really insightful class. I would only recommend if you are going to put the time and effort in, but it is interesting and one of the most valuable courses.
Lectures are very discussion based and majority is in groups. Lots of readings but she does a great job explaining concepts in class.

Dr. Georgis is an incredible prof who cares about students and truly wants you to learn. This is not just a class, it's a huge learning experience through reading, conversation, question-asking, and listening.
